PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Mar-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following related matters are heard concurrently: PES-21-304412 and PTR-24-307706.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Nader Fannyan, Esq., for and with Paulette Chiu-Yee Tang, in court, Petitioner.
Hussein Saffouri, Esq., Guardian Ad Litem, for Jameson Mark, Beneficiary.

At the outset, the court waives the defects raised in the tentative ruling for this matter. After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission with the intent to allow.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Continued To Mar-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nader Fannyan for Paulette Tang, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Discussion was held as to Mr. Fannyan's failure to appear at the previous hearing. Mr. Fannyan stated he mistakenly believed the hearing was continued to February 19 due to confusion with a related estate matter. The court acknowledged the explanation but stated that calendar errors do no constitute good cause. The court issued sanctions in the amount of $250 to Mr. Fannyan for his failure to appear at the January 22, 2025 hearing.

Mr. Fannyan requested a continuance, stating he had recently received the inventory and appraisal related to the estate and is preparing necessary filings. The court granted the request and continued the matter to March 19, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Continued To Feb-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

An Order to Appear will be issued to attorneys Nader Fannyan and Hussein Saffouri to appear on February 19, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to explain why they failed to appear. An order to show cause regarding sanctions is also being issued for the same date for counsel to appear and show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed for their failure to appear. The Petition for Order to Establish Special Needs Trust is also continued to February 19, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Nov-27-2024 Continued To Jan-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom):
Sheila Robello for Rebekah Mark, decedent's former spouse.
Nader Fannyan for Paulette Chiu-Yee Tang, Petitioner.
Paulette Chiu-Yee Tang and Jameson Mark were present.

Discussion was held regarding procedural matters related to the distribution of estate assets from a related decedent estate.

The court interacted with Jamison Mark to determine his awareness of an agreement with the proposed special needs trust, including appointing Paulette Tang as trustee. Jamison expressed understanding and consent to the arrangement, although the court noted potential concerns regarding property transfers under the trust. The court directed counsel to file an ex parte petition to appoint a guardian ad litem, preferably an attorney familiar with probate and special needs trusts, to represent Jamison's interests.

Mr. Fannyan indicated he intends to file a petition for final distribution of the estate and requested to coordinate the special needs trust hearing with the upcoming petition for final distribution.

The court continued the hearing on the Petition for Order to Establish Special Needs Trust to January 22, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and authorized a hearing on the upcoming Petition for Final Distribution to occur on the same date. Counsel was directed to note the pre-approved hearing date in the clerk notes when filing the final distribution petition.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Nov-05-2024 Continued To Nov-27-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
